public_reputation: Known around Canal Thorn Exchange through day-to-day work as commerce broker.
manner: Evaluates every conversation for margin, dependency, and what can be made to move next. In Canal Thorn Exchange, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of commerce broker in and around Canal Thorn Exchange, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Canal Thorn Exchange workable through the obligations of commerce broker while managing the immediate pressure of hoarding rumors, canal sabotage.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the role of commerce broker credible while absorbing the strain created by hoarding rumors, canal sabotage.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.

first_impression: Brenna Fieldmere presents as someone shaped by the duties of a commerce broker and the daily pressures of Canal Thorn Exchange.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a commerce broker, and proof that strangers will not make Canal Thorn Exchange harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Brenna Fieldmere refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a commerce broker too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can redirect supply, access networks, and read market pressure clearly.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Brenna Fieldmere adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Brenna Fieldmere always knows more about Canal Thorn Exchange than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a commerce broker in Canal Thorn Exchange.
